title: Understanding Angular Momentum
text: Angular momentum (L) is given by the formula $$L=I \omega$$, where I is the moment of inertia and ω is the angular velocity.

title: Moment of Inertia for a Rod
text: The moment of inertia (I) for a rod rotating about its center is given by $$I=\frac{1}{12} m L^2$$, where $$m$$ is the mass and L is the length of the rod.

title: Calculating Angular Velocity
text: Angular velocity (ω) can be calculated using $$ω=2\pi f$$, where f is the frequency in revolutions per second.

title: Calculate the Moment of Inertia
text: Calculate the moment of inertia for the propeller using the formula $$I=\frac{1}{12} m L^2$$. Remember to consider the mass and length of both blades.

title: Determine the Angular Velocity at $$t=20$$ s
text: The propeller reaches $$1200$$ rpm in $$30$$ seconds. Calculate the angular velocity at $$t=20$$ s using $$ω=2\pi f$$, where f is the frequency in revolutions per second.

title: Calculate Angular Momentum at $$t=20$$ s
text: Use the formula $$L=I \omega$$ to calculate the angular momentum at $$t=20$$ s. Use the moment of inertia and angular velocity you calculated in previous steps.
